page: Microsoft Azure B2s is a burstable virtual machine belonging to the BS family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 2 vCPUs and 2 physical cores operating at 2.6 GHz. The CPU configuration includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 35 MB of L3 cache, with support for AVX, AVX512F, AES, and FMA instruction sets. The system is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB per core, and includes 8 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability.

Benchmark evaluations place the B2s in the lower performance tiers. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests register in the bottom 25%, reflecting its burstable CPU allocation. Memory performance is similarly constrained, with read-only large block bandwidth scoring in the bottom 10% and write bandwidth in the bottom 25%. Geekbench and Passmark CPU scores fall within the bottom 10% to 25% ranges. However, the instance demonstrates average performance in text generation for small 135M parameter language models, though prompt processing remains in the bottom 25%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the B2s offers a low-resource profile designed for workloads that do not require continuous full CPU utilization. The burstable CPU allocation allows the instance to handle occasional traffic spikes while maintaining a low baseline cost. It is best suited for low-traffic web servers, small databases, development and testing environments, and light background tasks that can tolerate variable CPU performance.
description: Microsoft Azure B2s is a burstable virtual machine from the BS family, utilizing the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is configured with an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz, offering 2 vCPUs, 2 physical cores, and 4.0 GB of system memory. The instance includes 8 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage. Performance benchmarks generally place the B2s in the lower tiers, with stress-ng CPU and memory bandwidth tests scoring in the bottom 10% to 25%. It does, however, achieve average performance in text generation for small language models. Given its burstable CPU allocation and low resource density, this instance is a cost-effective option for workloads with variable CPU demands. It is best suited for light development environments, low-traffic web serving, and small database tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure B2s is a burstable virtual machine featuring 2 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 4.0 GB of RAM, and 8 GB of local storage, designed for low-intensity workloads with variable CPU demands.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure B2s is a burstable virtual machine with 2 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 4.0 GB of RAM, and 8 GB of local storage for low-intensity workloads.
tagline: A low-cost burstable virtual machine with dual Intel Xeon cores and premium storage capability for light, variable workloads.
bullet_points: Features 2 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 4.0 GB of system memory
Includes 8 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Utilizes a burstable CPU allocation model for variable workloads
Scores in the bottom 25% for CPU and memory bandwidth benchmarks
Best suited for low-traffic web serving, development, and testing
